rspStmt.AuthEnty: Kaplan Centre for Jewish Studies and Research

prodStmt.fundAg: South African Jewish Board of Deputies

serStmt.serInfo: Institute for Jewish Policy Research and the Kaplan Centre for Jewish Studies. Survey of Jewish South Africans 1998 [dataset]. Version 1. London: IJPR. Cape Town: Kaplan Centre [producers], 1999. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2021. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/W4Y8-W546

verStmt.version: v1.1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: The dataset was provided to DataFirst by the Kaplan Centre for Jewish Studies in March 2021.

stdyInfo.abstract: The survey was initiated in 1998 by the IJPR and the Kaplan Centre and conducted using face-to-face interviews with a sample of adults from Jewish families in Cape Town, Durban, Johannesburg, and Pretoria.

sumDscr.geogCover: The survey covered selected Jewish households in Cape Town, Durban, Johannesburg, and Pretoria.
sumDscr.universe: The target population of the survey consists of Jewish South Africans.

stdyInfo.notes: The survey collected background data on Jewish households in the four cities. Data was collected on: citizenship, education levels, home language, home ownership, marital status, migration history, and number of children. Data on political and social attitudes of respondents was also collected. Collection of data specifically related to being Jewish in South Africa was the main purpose of the survey. This included data on: Childrens' enrollment in Jewish schools and the respondent's attitudes to these schools; attitudes to Israel, religious beliefs, practices and experiences; attitudes to and experiences of anti-semitism, the Jewish community and Jewish organisations.

dataColl.resInstru: A single household questionnaire was used for the survey

useStmt.citReq: Institute for Jewish Policy Research and the Kaplan Centre for Jewish Studies. Survey of Jewish South Africans 1998 [dataset]. Version 1. London: IJPR. Cape Town: Kaplan Centre [producers], 1999. Cape Town. DataFirst [distributor], 2021. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/W4Y8-W546

catgry.labl: Single (never married, not living w. partner)

var.name: ifnjpri
var.labl: Non-J primary gov. school - eldest kid

catgry.labl: About the same as 5 yrs ago

var.name: AIDS5
var.labl: AIDS should be able to work

var.name: atwomjob
var.labl: Family suffers if women work FT

var.name: atjuggle
var.labl: Women have to work to supplement income

var.name: POL94MOR
var.labl: [Other parties voted for in 94] merged into pol94

var.name: lifeup
var.labl: Quality of life will improve over 5 yrs

var.name: sunpothr
var.labl: Other Sunday papers twice a month

var.labl: Country mother born in (not in HH)

var.name: bornfath
var.labl: Country father born in (not in HH)

catgry.labl: Born J, but do not think of self as J
Aware of J, don't think of it often
Quite strongly J, but equally conscious of other things
Extremely conscious of J, v. important

var.name: BELIEF1
var.labl: Jews depend on Jews in crisis

var.name: BELIEF5
var.labl: Belief in God central to being Jew

var.name: jmarluck
var.labl: If married, purely by chance a Jew

var.name: jcomkids
var.labl: J partner only important if want kids

var.name: holoid
var.labl: Holocaust should be core of young J ID

catgry.labl: Inspired word of God, not literal
From ordinary butcher but no pork

var.name: koshexp
var.labl: If cheaper more J would keep Kosher

var.name: usyngo
var.labl: Often attend shul in past year

var.name: ifwelcom
var.labl: J community welcome nature to singles

catgry.labl: Not really looking, but wouldn't mind

var.name: jcomconv
var.labl: Rabbis should be more welcoming to NJ partners

var.name: jcomsing
var.labl: J community singles pressured to marry

var.name: jcomwon
var.labl: Women get raw deal in Judaism

var.name: jcomsynl
var.labl: Shul organisers make others feel outsiders

var.name: jcomoutm
var.labl: Do everything to prevent child intermarriage

var.name: jcomlone
var.labl: J community no place for singles

var.name: jsastay
var.labl: Big SA J community in 20 yrs

var.name: jsaisl
var.labl: LT future for Jews in Israel

var.name: jsadati
var.labl: Orthodox only will survive as recognisable Jews

var.name: jsfutur
var.labl: Most J under 30 do NOT see future in SA

catgry.labl: Not converted but lives as Jewish

var.name: attform
var.labl: Attended formal J lessons from parent/relative

var.name: expisrl
var.labl: Did Israel experience during school age

var.name: jedimp
var.labl: Important all J kids attend formal J edc

var.name: jedknow
var.labl: More yrs in J edc = more knowledge

var.name: jedjid
var.labl: More yrs J edc = stronger J ID

var.name: jedjlife
var.labl: More yrs J edc = greater commitment to J life as adult

var.name: pamoreth
var.labl: Difference in J ID between parents

var.name: pamorgod
var.labl: Difference in parental belief in God

var.name: POL94_3
var.labl: 94 political votes, ANC, National Party and DEmocratic Party
